title: Run rootless containers on SSSD system == "cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid"
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

I am unable to run a rootless container on a RHEL 7.5 workstation using SSSD for authentication. This happens when I run any container on my workstation. Namespace mappings exist for my login name in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`.

This is similar to #1147.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. `podman pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora:latest`
2. `podman run --rm -it registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora bash`

**Describe the results you received:**

```sh
$ podman --log-level=debug run --rm -it registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora bash

DEBU[0000] Using tmp dir /run/user/43228/libpod/tmp

DEBU[0001] Network is already cleaned up, skipping...

DEBU[0001] Cleaning up container e9c9760c99063f56f69a3306451ac856b1fbb119fcccbdbadb26af4f91743ac2 
DEBU[0001] Network is already cleaned up, skipping...   
DEBU[0001] Container e9c9760c99063f56f69a3306451ac856b1fbb119fcccbdbadb26af4f91743ac2 storage is already unmounted, skipping... 
DEBU[0001] Container e9c9760c99063f56f69a3306451ac856b1fbb119fcccbdbadb26af4f91743ac2 storage is already unmounted, skipping... 
ERRO[0001] cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers
: OCI runtime error
```

**Describe the results you expected:**

```sh
$ podman run --rm -it registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora bash
[root@000000000 /]#
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```sh
podman version 1.4.4
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Physical workstation, no virtualization used. Also using updated `newxidmap` COPR package from `shadow-utils`.

comments.body:
`ERRO[0001] cannot specify gid= mount options for unmapped gid in rootless containers` is coming out of `runc` by the look of things. There aren't any group settings listed, though, and no warnings about no GIDs being available in /etc/subgid.

@giuseppe Mind taking a look?
I remember that being an issue in runc.  What version of runc are you using?  Could you try to update it?

@giuseppe Updating the `runc` to latest upstream (at time of writing, `v1.0.0-rc8`) resolved the issue. I can now run rootless containers on my workstation. Thanks!

Closing. :clapper:
